analyzers.description: Aggregated domain-aware semantic checks for SattLine programs and libraries
analyzers.name: SattLine semantics

analyzers.description: MMSWriteVar/MMSReadVar inventory with OPC and MES validation checks
analyzers.name: MMS interface mappings

analyzers.description: Parallel-branch write race and structural dead-path detection
analyzers.name: SFC checks

analyzers.description: Local variables hiding outer or global names
analyzers.name: Variable shadowing

analyzers.description: AST-visible checks from the application engineering spec
analyzers.name: Engineering spec compliance

analyzers.description: Cross-module duplicate tag, duplicate condition, priority, and latch-style alarm checks
analyzers.name: Alarm integrity

analyzers.description: Detect recipe and engineering parameter modules that do not resolve a required startup value
analyzers.name: Initial value validation

analyzers.description: Detect inconsistent naming styles for variables, modules, and instances across the analyzed target
analyzers.name: Naming consistency

analyzers.description: Detect repeated module names that have drifted structurally beyond datecode-only changes
analyzers.name: Version drift

analyzers.description: Cross-module tracing for shutdown and emergency signal propagation
analyzers.name: Safety paths

analyzers.description: Cross-module taint tracing from external inputs to safety-critical sinks
analyzers.name: Taint paths

analyzers.description: Explicit boolean defaults that can enable logic or bypass safeguards at startup
analyzers.name: Unsafe defaults

analyzers.description: Constant-condition and unreachable-path detection across branches
analyzers.name: Lightweight dataflow

rules.description: Conditions that always evaluate to false at a particular program point.
Conditions that always evaluate to true at a particular program point.
The same alarm tag or condition should not be configured with conflicting priorities or severities.
Parameter mappings whose connected datatypes are incompatible across module boundaries.
Writes whose value is overwritten before any later read can observe it.
Alarm sources should not reuse the same condition without deliberate deduplication.
Alarm sources should not reuse the same tag across the analyzed target.
Sibling modules with the same case-insensitive name.
Transitions in the same sequence branch that normalize to the same guard logic.
Complex datatypes that appear duplicated by structure.
External MES, operator, or sensor inputs that propagate into safety-critical sinks across modules.
Root globals whose access never escapes a single module subtree and can be localized.
Root globals that act as implicit interfaces across multiple module paths.
Root globals that are read or written by many distinct module paths and become highly shared coordination points.
Configured mutually exclusive SFC steps can become active at the same time.
Boolean values that are set on some branches or steps without a matching False write on the complementary path.
Min_/Max_ parameter mappings that do not line up by base name.
Recipe and engineering parameter modules should resolve a startup value through a default or an explicitly initialized mapping.
Configured SFC steps should initialize their required state in the enter block.
Configured SFC steps should clean up their required state in the exit block.
Case-insensitive declaration name collisions within a scope.
Alarm variables that are only forced true and never explicitly cleared can latch unexpectedly.
Variables that are written but never subsequently read.
Parallel SFC branches that write to the same variable or field.
Procedure status outputs should be checked in control logic instead of being ignored or shown only in UI.
Variable reads that can occur before any definite assignment on the current path.
Writable declarations that are only ever read.
Reset-state writes that are missing or incomplete across sequence flow.
Implicit same-scan reads of a state variable after a write should use :NEW when they rely on the updated value.
Reads of :OLD after a same-scan write where the expression still relies on the previous-scan snapshot.
:OLD references are read-only and cannot be used as write targets or out-parameters.
Conditions that compare a symbol to itself and collapse to a constant result.
Local declarations that shadow outer or global names.
Configured SFC steps can inherit stale state from earlier steps when required enter writes are missing.
Parameter mappings whose string-like datatypes are incompatible.
Transitions with guards that simplify to false and therefore can never fire.
Transitions with guards that simplify to true and therefore always fire when reached.
Variables that are only consumed through graphics or interact display wiring.
Safety-critical signals that are written but never consumed across the analyzed target.
Unexpected non-module nodes under the submodule tree.
Parameter mappings that target undeclared module parameters.
Branches that cannot execute because control-flow facts make them impossible.
Sequence nodes that cannot execute because an earlier node terminates the branch.
Sequence nodes that cannot execute after an earlier terminating node in the same branch.
Transitions that can never fire because an earlier node structurally terminates the branch.
Boolean defaults that enable logic or bypass safeguards from startup.
Datatype fields that are never used across the analyzed target.
Declared variables that are never read or written.
Variables whose written values are read internally but never reach a root-visible output.
BasePicture code must stay inside frame modules.
MES_BatchControl Max_TRY must resolve to the required value.
MES_BatchControl instances must use the required instance name.
MES_BatchControl Repeat_TRY must resolve to the required value.
OPMessage instances must not enable UseSignature=True.
Sequence steps must use the required engineering-spec naming prefix.
All transitions must be named.
Transitions must use the required engineering-spec naming prefix.
